message:
test(fcp-core): pin DeviceSelector + ObjectPlacementPolicy serde shape (flywheel_connectors-xmri2)

Bead asks for ConnectorTopology serde JSON+CBOR roundtrip. fcp-core
has no type literally named ConnectorTopology; the topology-shaped
surface deciding "which mesh nodes can host which objects/connectors"
splits across DeviceSelector (object.rs:154, 5-variant externally-
tagged enum Tag/Class/NodeId/Zone/HasCapability), ObjectPlacementPolicy
(object.rs:180, 6-field placement struct), and MeshPlacementHint
(object.rs:165, already pinned by mesh_placement_hint_serde_ordering.rs).
This test pins the unpinned DeviceSelector + ObjectPlacementPolicy
surface.

Coverage:
- DeviceSelector per-variant JSON shape pinned (externally-tagged
  single-key form: Tag with String, Class with String, NodeId with
  u64, Zone with canonical zone-id string, HasCapability with String)
- JSON round-trip per variant preserves payload (including u64::MAX
  for NodeId)
- CBOR round-trip per variant preserves payload via JSON-equivalence
  (DeviceSelector doesn't derive PartialEq)
- CBOR encoding shape: single-key Map keyed on PascalCase variant
  name with payload as Text/Integer per variant
- PascalCase canonical: snake_case + kebab-case + unknown outer keys
  rejected (drift sentinel)
- ObjectPlacementPolicy 6-field JSON shape pinned (min_nodes,
  max_node_fraction_bps, preferred_devices, excluded_devices,
  target_coverage_bps, min_source_diversity)
- JSON + CBOR round-trip preserves all 6 fields
- Default values when deserializing partial JSON: min_source_diversity
  defaults to 0; preferred_devices and excluded_devices default to
  empty Vec via #[serde(default)]
- Nested DeviceSelector list with all 5 variant kinds preserved
  through policy round-trip with order preservation
- Cross-format consistency: JSON and CBOR decode ObjectPlacementPolicy
  to the same field values

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
